articles.title: All the evidence revealed so far in Musk v. Altman
articles.summary: The Musk v. Altman trial reveals evidence including emails and documents from OpenAI's founding. Key findings show Musk drafted OpenAI's mission, Nvidia provided crucial computing resources, and early tensions existed between founders.

articles.title: Pentagon strikes classified AI deals with OpenAI, Google, and Nvidia — but not Anthropic
articles.summary: The Pentagon has signed classified AI deals with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, xAI, and Reflection, but excluded Anthropic citing supply-chain risks.

articles.title: Elon Musk had a bad week in court
articles.summary: Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging the company stole nonprofit status, appears unlikely to succeed. His courtroom testimony reportedly did not go well despite initiating the trial himself.

articles.title: Replit’s Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why he’d rather not sell
articles.summary: Replit CEO Amjad Masad discussed the company's future at TechCrunch's StrictlyVC event, addressing speculation about acquisition pressures following Cursor's reported SpaceX deal negotiations.

articles.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
articles.summary: Meta acquired humanoid robotics startup Assured Robot Intelligence to enhance its AI capabilities for robotic systems and advance its humanoid AI development goals.

articles.title: Did you know you can’t steal a charity? Don’t worry. Elon Musk will remind you.
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming the company betrayed its nonprofit mission by converting to a for-profit model under Sam Altman's leadership.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud amid growing AI application demand.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has sparked developer backlash. Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers similar AI coding capabilities locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt helped the startup attract top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
articles.summary: Ubuntu and Canonical servers have been offline for over 24 hours due to a sustained cross-border cyberattack, disrupting OS updates and communications following a major vulnerability disclosure.

articles.title: The most severe Linux threat to surface in years catches the world flat-footed
articles.summary: Publicly released exploit code for a critical Linux vulnerability granting root access affects virtually all Linux releases, forcing urgent patching efforts across data centers and devices worldwide.

articles.title: Why a recent supply-chain attack singled out security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden
articles.summary: Security firm Checkmarx suffered multiple supply-chain attacks within six weeks, including compromised GitHub accounts delivering malware to customers and a ransomware attack from hackers seeking notoriety.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his trial against OpenAI, claiming he was deceived by Sam Altman and Greg Brockman. He admitted xAI uses OpenAI's models for training while warning AI poses existential risks.

articles.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
articles.summary: AI expands cybersecurity attack surfaces and complexity, requiring security to be rethought from the ground up rather than layered onto existing systems.

articles.title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
articles.summary: Companies are controlling their own data to customize AI while balancing data ownership with safe, high-quality information flow. AI factories enable scale, sustainability, and governance, making data control a strategic priority for enterprises and governments.

articles.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
articles.summary: AI evaluation has become a significant computational bottleneck as models grow larger. Companies now spend substantial resources testing and validating AI systems, rivaling training compute costs.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs represent an advancement in large language model architecture and training techniques. The article explores the foundational methods and design principles behind building these improved language models.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra joins Hugging Face Inference Providers, expanding AI model deployment options. This partnership enables developers to access diverse inference solutions through Hugging Face's unified platform.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential impacts. The conversation explores upcoming developments and opportunities in the AI field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative capabilities. These AI solutions help companies streamline content creation and imagination-driven tasks.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft releases its farm-of-the-future toolkit as open source, enabling agricultural innovation through accessible technology and AI-driven farming solutions.

articles.title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
articles.summary: Google Translate celebrates its 20th anniversary, evolving from a 2006 AI experiment to support nearly 250 languages today. The company shares fun facts highlighting the tool's development and expansion.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.summary: Google and Kaggle are offering a 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with open registration. This Vibe Coding Course provides hands-on training in AI agent development.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Gemini offers AI-powered tips for organizing physical and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox management, and seasonal chores.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. This innovation aims to enhance clinical care through AI collaboration with medical professionals.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with the Republic of Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo enables resilient distributed AI training by allowing independent local updates across multiple nodes before synchronization, improving fault tolerance and reducing communication overhead.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #242 discusses major AI developments including OpenAI's improved ChatGPT image model with better text accuracy, Qwen 3.6 Max, and Kimi-K2.6, covering recent industry breakthroughs.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family while discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia's DLSS 5 uses generative AI as a real-time filter to enhance video game graphics with photorealism. The technology extends beyond gaming applications.

articles.title: SAP: How enterprise AI governance secures profit margins
articles.summary: SAP emphasizes that enterprise AI governance ensures profit margins through deterministic control rather than statistical approximations. Organizations must prioritize precision, governance, and scalability when deploying AI in production environments.

articles.title: Per-token AI charges come to GitHub Copilot
articles.summary: GitHub Copilot is switching from flat-rate subscriptions to per-token pricing starting June 1, 2026, aligning with standard API pricing models based on input and output tokens used.

articles.title: What LG and NVIDIA’s talks reveal about the future of physical AI
articles.summary: LG and NVIDIA are exploring partnerships in physical AI, data centers, and mobility, revealing the massive infrastructure investments needed to deploy autonomous systems beyond simulation environments.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, destination understanding, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study finds that AI coding assistant Claude Code inadvertently publishes sensitive API keys to public code repositories, posing security risks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, posing significant supply chain risks.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for concise answers but reduces their ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems, revealing a significant tradeoff between speed and generalization.

articles.title: A New NVIDIA Research Shows Speculative Decoding in NeMo RL Achieves 1.8× Rollout Generation Speedup at 8B and Projects 2.5× End-to-End Speedup at 235B
articles.summary: NVIDIA integrated speculative decoding into NeMo RL to accelerate rollout generation, achieving 1.8× speedup at 8B and projecting 2.5× speedup at 235B while preserving output distribution.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation of End-to-End Brain Decoding from MEG Signals Using NeuralSet and Deep Learning for Predicting Linguistic Features
articles.summary: Tutorial demonstrates decoding linguistic features from MEG brain signals using neuroAI pipeline and deep learning. Researchers build an end-to-end system with CNNs to predict word length from neural activity patterns.

articles.title: Meta Introduces Autodata: An Agentic Framework That Turns AI Models into Autonomous Data Scientists for High-Quality Training Data Creation
articles.summary: Meta introduced Autodata, an agentic framework that deploys AI agents as autonomous data scientists to iteratively build and refine training datasets without human annotation. Testing shows it outperforms traditional synthetic data generation on complex scientific reasoning tasks.

articles.title: T-Mobile is giving 5G customers Hulu and Netflix subscriptions, free - here's how to get them
articles.summary: T-Mobile now includes free Hulu and Netflix subscriptions (with ads) for customers on Experience Beyond or Go5G Next plans.

articles.title: 5 last-minute gifts your mom will actually like for Mother's Day (and all are on sale)
articles.summary: A curated list of discounted Mother's Day gift ideas featuring practical gadgets and cozy items that have been approved by staff members' mothers.

articles.title: Samsung will give you a free 32-inch Odyssey monitor right now - how to qualify
articles.summary: Samsung is offering a buy-one-get-one deal on monitors, giving customers a free 32-inch Odyssey monitor with purchase. Check Samsung's current promotion terms to qualify.

articles.title: Effective KV Compression with TurboQuant
articles.summary: Google launched TurboQuant, an algorithmic suite for quantizing and compressing large language models and vector search engines used in RAG systems.

articles.title: Building AI Agents in Python with Pydantic AI
articles.summary: Pydantic AI enables developers to build AI agents in Python using structured data validation. The framework simplifies creating intelligent systems by combining Pydantic's type safety with AI capabilities.

articles.title: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Developer’s Guide
articles.summary: Guide on context engineering for AI agents, helping developers optimize prompt design and information structuring to improve agent performance and reliability.

articles.title: How to Get Hired in the AI Era
articles.summary: The article discusses what hiring managers look for in junior candidates in the AI field, highlighting qualities that help candidates stand out in competitive job markets.

articles.title: Churn Without Fragmentation: How a Party-Label Bug Reversed My Headline Finding
articles.summary: A data quality case study reveals how party-label inconsistencies in English local election data affected analytical findings. Proper categorical normalization and metric validation are essential for accurate voter volatility analysis.

articles.title: Ghost: A Database for Our Times?
articles.summary: Ghost is introduced as the first database specifically designed for AI Agents, representing a new approach to data management tailored to modern artificial intelligence systems.

articles.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
articles.summary: OpenClaw agents have become a phenomenon with over 100,000 GitHub stars by early 2026, enabling organizations to build specialized AI systems using open models and training techniques on NVIDIA platforms.

articles.title: It’s Gonna Be May: 16 Games Hit the Cloud This Month, With More NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Power
articles.summary: 16 games are joining NVIDIA GeForce NOW in May, including AAA launches like Forza Horizon 6 and 007 First Light. GeForce RTX 5080 power expands to the Install-to-Play library.

articles.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Model, Unifying Vision, Audio and Language for up to 9x More Efficient AI Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a unified multimodal AI model combining vision, audio, and language capabilities for up to 9x more efficient AI agents with faster, smarter responses.

articles.title: AWS Transform now automates BI migration to Amazon Quick in days
articles.summary: AWS Transform automates migration of BI dashboards to Amazon QuickSight, reducing migration timelines from months to days while preserving domain knowledge and security rules.

articles.title: Reinforcement fine-tuning with LLM-as-a-judge
articles.summary: Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RFT) uses automated reward signals to align LLMs efficiently, replacing costly manual labeling. Methods like RLVR and RLAIF employ verifiable rewards or LLM-as-a-judge to score generations and guide model alignment.

articles.title: AWS Generative AI Model Agility Solution: A comprehensive guide to migrating LLMs for generative AI production
articles.summary: AWS presents a comprehensive guide for migrating large language models in production environments. The solution addresses technical challenges while maintaining model agility and minimizing operational disruptions.

articles.title: Speed Up Unreal Engine NNE Inference with NVIDIA TensorRT for RTX Runtime
articles.summary: NVIDIA TensorRT now accelerates neural network inference in Unreal Engine's NNE for RTX, enabling faster image quality enhancement, denoising, and neural rendering in real-time graphics.

articles.title: Build AI-Powered Games with NVIDIA DLSS 4.5, RTX, and Unreal Engine 5
articles.summary: NVIDIA releases DLSS 4.5 for game developers, featuring Dynamic Multi Frame Generation, Multi Frame Generation 6X, and an improved Super Resolution transformer model for Unreal Engine 5 integration.

articles.title: How to Build, Run, and Scale High-Quality Creator Workflows in ComfyUI
articles.summary: ComfyUI enables creative teams to build automated, repeatable generative AI workflows that compress hours of manual work into efficient pipelines for asset production.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations perceive and interact with social media advertisements across various platforms, exploring their distinct attitudes and behaviors toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce carbon footprint and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background leveraged their unique perspective to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences drive breakthrough innovations in XR.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient shaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. The work analyzes LLM decision-making processes through feature attribution to enhance AI safety and trustworthiness.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems using encoders to map objects to images, with noise models to quantify measurement quality and distinguish between objects.
